page.title: Boxing Day Yacht Charter in Marbella: A Relaxed Post-Christmas Cruise

data.meta_description: Book a Boxing Day yacht charter in Marbella for a relaxed post-Christmas cruise. Prices from €749/2h. Departs Puerto Banús. Inclusions: skipper, drinks, snacks.
data.summary: Escape the holiday rush with a Boxing Day yacht charter in Marbella. Cruise the Costa del Sol from Puerto Banús with all-inclusive pricing, a licensed skipper, and festive drinks on board.
key_facts: Charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella, on Boxing Day (26 December).
Prices from €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39) up to €4,719 for 4 hours (Mangusta 80).
Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%).
Onboard drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, plus light snacks.
Fleet ranges from 6.4m speedboats (5 pax) to 29m superyachts (12 pax).
Average sea temperature in December: 16°C; air temps 14–18°C.
Book via WhatsApp for any boat not listed with set pricing.
No hidden fees – all taxes and crew included in the quoted price.

faq.q: What is a Boxing Day yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: It’s a private boat rental on 26 December, departing from Puerto Banús. You cruise the Costa del Sol with family or friends, enjoy onboard drinks and snacks, and relax after Christmas Day. All charters include a skipper, fuel, insurance, and VAT.

faq.q: How much does a Boxing Day yacht charter cost?
faq.a: Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. The Mangusta 80 (Tier B) is €4,719 for a minimum 4 hours, with a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no extra charges unless you request catering or additional water toys.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The included bar is non-negotiable, but you can supplement it. Just let us know in advance if you have special dietary requirements or want to order a catered menu.

faq.q: Is the weather suitable for a boat trip in December?
faq.a: Marbella enjoys mild winters. Air temperatures on Boxing Day typically range from 14°C to 18°C, with sea temperatures around 16°C. Sunny days are common, and the sea is usually calm. Bring a light jacket – the breeze can be cool offshore.

faq.q: Which boats are available on Boxing Day?
faq.a: Our full fleet is available, from the 6.4m Mariah SX21 (5 pax) to the 29m Ferretti 94 (12 pax). All depart from Puerto Banús. Popular choices for groups include the Astondoa 40 (9 pax) and the Mangusta 80 (12 pax).

faq.q: How do I book a Boxing Day charter?
faq.a: You can book directly via WhatsApp through our website. For Tier A and B boats, prices are listed. For all other boats, send us a message with your preferred vessel, number of guests, and desired duration – we’ll confirm availability and pricing.

We offer a full refund if you cancel at least 48 hours before departure. Within 48 hours, we may retain a deposit unless we can rebook the slot. Weather-related cancellations are fully refundable. Check our terms at booking.

data.body_html:
Boxing Day offers a rare chance to escape the post-Christmas lull. Instead of leftovers and repeats on TV, step aboard a private boat in Puerto Banús and cruise the Costa del Sol under winter sun. Our Boxing Day yacht charter in Marbella combines the comfort of a heated cabin with open-air decks, festive drinks, and a licensed skipper who handles everything. Prices start at €749 for two hours on a 12.5m Astondoa 40, including all taxes, fuel, and a bar stocked with cava, beer, wine, and soft drinks. Whether you want a short coastal hop or a full-day excursion to Cabopino or Estepona, this is a relaxed, no-hassle way to spend 26 December.

Why Choose a Boxing Day Yacht Charter in Marbella?

Marbella’s microclimate delivers mild December days – average highs of 18°C and sea temperatures around 16°C. The marina is quieter than summer, so you’ll find berths available and fewer boats on the water. A Marbella yacht charter on Boxing Day gives you the freedom to set your own pace: anchor off Cala del Faro for a swim (wetsuit recommended), motor along the Golden Mile for skyline views, or simply relax on deck with a glass of cava. The skipper handles navigation, anchoring, and safety, leaving you to enjoy the day without planning a thing.

Our Fleet for Boxing Day Cruises

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús. For small groups, the 6.4m Mariah SX21 (5 pax) or 8m Dubhe (5 pax) offer a nimble, budget-friendly option. Families of up to eight enjoy the 11.5m Bandido (8 pax) or 12m Fairline Targa (10 pax). For larger parties, the 17.6m Azimut 58 Flybridge and 24m Mangusta 80 (both 12 pax) provide spacious saloons and sundecks. The 29m Ferretti 94 (12 pax) is our flagship, with multiple lounges and a flybridge. Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and VAT – no hidden extras.

Boxing Day Charter Pricing – What You Pay

Our pricing is transparent. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours, €1,299 for 4 hours, or €2,299 for 8 hours. Tier B (Mangusta 80, including grey and white variants) is €4,719 for a minimum 4 hours, with a jet ski included free. All other boats – from the Azimut 58 to the Ferretti 94 – are priced on request via WhatsApp. The price always includes skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. You pay exactly what you see.

What’s Included On Board

  • Licensed, English-speaking skipper
  • Fuel for the full charter duration
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava
  • Light snacks (olives, crisps, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment (life jackets, first aid kit)
  • Spanish IVA (21%) – no tax surprise at the end

You are welcome to bring your own food and extra drinks. If you want a catered menu or a specific wine list, just ask – we can arrange it at cost.

Suggested Boxing Day Itineraries

2-hour cruise: Head west from Puerto Banús towards the Golden Mile, passing Marbella Marina and the beach clubs. Return via the same route with views of La Concha mountain.

4-hour trip: Sail east to Cabopino or west to Cala del Faro. Anchor for a swim or a leisurely lunch. The skipper will choose a calm spot based on wind and sea conditions.

8-hour full day: Go as far as Estepona (west) or Sotogrande (further west), or east to Cala Cortés and beyond. Stop for a beachside meal at a chiringuito (many stay open through winter). Return to Puerto Banús as the sun sets around 18:15.

What to Wear and Bring

December temperatures on the water are cooler than summer. Bring a warm jacket or fleece, sunglasses, sunscreen (the sun is still strong), and a hat. If you plan to swim, a wetsuit or rash vest is advisable – the sea is 16°C. The boat has a heated cabin, so you can warm up between deck sessions. Cameras and phones are fine, but a waterproof case is wise for spray.

How to Book Your Boxing Day Charter

Booking is straightforward. For Tier A and B boats, you can reserve directly via WhatsApp with the price listed. For all other vessels, send us a message with your preferred boat, number of guests, and desired duration. We’ll confirm availability and send a quote. Payment is by bank transfer or card. We recommend booking at least a week in advance for Boxing Day, as availability is limited. For more ideas, read our Boxing Day yacht charter Marbella guide.

Frequently asked questions

What is a Boxing Day yacht charter in Marbella?

It’s a private boat rental on 26 December, departing from Puerto Banús. You cruise the Costa del Sol with family or friends, enjoy onboard drinks and snacks, and relax after Christmas Day. All charters include a skipper, fuel, insurance, and VAT.

How much does a Boxing Day yacht charter cost?

Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. The Mangusta 80 (Tier B) is €4,719 for a minimum 4 hours, with a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

What is included in the charter price?

Every booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no extra charges unless you request catering or additional water toys.

Can I bring my own food and drinks?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The included bar is non-negotiable, but you can supplement it. Just let us know in advance if you have special dietary requirements or want to order a catered menu.

Is the weather suitable for a boat trip in December?

Marbella enjoys mild winters. Air temperatures on Boxing Day typically range from 14°C to 18°C, with sea temperatures around 16°C. Sunny days are common, and the sea is usually calm. Bring a light jacket – the breeze can be cool offshore.

Which boats are available on Boxing Day?

Our full fleet is available, from the 6.4m Mariah SX21 (5 pax) to the 29m Ferretti 94 (12 pax). All depart from Puerto Banús. Popular choices for groups include the Astondoa 40 (9 pax) and the Mangusta 80 (12 pax).

How do I book a Boxing Day charter?

You can book directly via WhatsApp through our website. For Tier A and B boats, prices are listed. For all other boats, send us a message with your preferred vessel, number of guests, and desired duration – we’ll confirm availability and pricing.

What is the cancellation policy?

We offer a full refund if you cancel at least 48 hours before departure. Within 48 hours, we may retain a deposit unless we can rebook the slot. Weather-related cancellations are fully refundable. Check our terms at booking.
